docs.text: Welcome to DAT Documentation! In here I'll try to add all the functionality of DAT add-on, and what you can do with it. Remember that this is a program I made to help the community, and it's not in any way connected with DungeonAlchemist™ officially.
docs.title: Getting Started

docs.text: About DAT Docs provides documentation for DATools operators and usage. Overview This documentation site covers installation, usage, and operator reference information. Purpose To help users understand and use the DATools features effectively.
docs.title: About

docs.text: Operators Tools at your hands What are Operators? An operator, in Blender, is a script you can recall no matter the panel. This means that if you make an operator, for example to open a box that says "Hello World!", if the CONTEXT (basically the window) allows it, it will run. Are there any in DAT? Most of the custom scripts I've wrote for DAT are operators. You can use the Blender search bar to find DATools everywhere, no matter the panel you're in. This way you can build your own UI, add DATools to other add-on's panels, or run them with favorites.
docs.title: Operators

docs.text: Tools at your hands
